executive_summary: This 20-minute playtest session covered exploration of the Blighted Village basement, combat with Mud Mephits in the Sunlit Wetlands, and a major confrontation with Kagha in the Druid Sanctuary. The session transitioned from lighthearted emergent social play into a cycle of high-stakes tactical combat, ending with successful inventory management and character customization after a significant boss encounter.

key_findings.evidence_summary: At 10:14, the party experienced a total wipe against Kagha. Upon checking the load menu at 10:17, the player discovered the last save was from 'an hour ago.' Sentiment plummeted to -9 as the player stated, 'An hour of my life is gone.'
key_findings.finding: Infrequent autosave triggers before major narrative combat encounters lead to extreme progress loss and player frustration.
key_findings.recommended_action: Add a mandatory autosave trigger at the threshold of the Druid Sanctuary or upon the initiation of the Kagha confrontation dialogue.

key_findings.evidence_summary: At 6:08 and 8:04, combat with numerous Mud Mephits caused significant pacing issues. The player expressed frustration with slow AI movement ('watch them move three inches') and at 8:22, admitted to checking TikTok because the turn duration was too long.
key_findings.finding: High enemy/summon counts with slow individual AI turn processing induce player disengagement and secondary-screen browsing.
key_findings.recommended_action: Implement simultaneous turn processing for clusters of identical low-level enemies and summons.

key_findings.evidence_summary: At 16:41, co-op players argued over the Haste Helm. One player equipped it while another insisted it was for the 'Wizard of Waterdeep' quest ('You're supposed to feed Gale'). This caused several minutes of menu toggling and back-and-forth inventory trading.
key_findings.finding: Co-op players struggle to distinguish between equippable upgrades and narrative-critical items meant for companion consumption.
key_findings.recommended_action: Add a distinct UI tag or color-coded border to items that satisfy active companion quest requirements (e.g., 'Gale-Eligible').

key_findings.evidence_summary: At 18:31, while looting in the Toll House basement, the camera failed to dither out the ceiling geometry, leaving the player in a black screen. The player stopped moving and asked, 'I'm getting shut in here. Hello?' reflecting perceived soft-lock anxiety.
key_findings.finding: Camera occlusion failure in tight vertical interiors creates player confusion and the false perception of being trapped.
key_findings.recommended_action: Audit ceiling transparency/dithering triggers in the Risen Road Toll House basement to ensure the player character remains visible.

priority_actions: Place a mandatory autosave trigger before the Kagha confrontation dialogue starts (10:17).
Enable simultaneous AI movement turns for groups of 3+ identical small enemies like Mud Mephits (6:08).
Apply a 'Quest Item' tag to magical artifacts required for Gale's 'Wizard of Waterdeep' quest line in the shared inventory (16:41).
Fix camera occlusion logic for the Toll House basement rooms to prevent geometry black-outs (18:31).
Add a subtle visual glimmer to the 'tree cleft' quest objective to reduce circular pathing at 8:35.
cross_dimensional_insight: The high delight scores recorded for character customization (18:59, sentiment 9) and emergent social humor (13:21, sentiment 9) acted as a critical 'frustration buffer.' Despite losing an hour of progress at 10:17, the player’s deep investment in their character’s aesthetic identity—evidenced by the enthusiastic reaction to armor dye—fueled a rapid transition back into focused engagement for the rematch.
